title: Data Cleansing Rules + Freshness Model v0

duplicate_slugs.action: exclude conflicting records from snapshot candidates until reviewed

duplicate_canonical_urls.action: choose no winner automatically when canonical URLs collide across slugs
duplicate_canonical_urls.public_output: emit only records already resolved in reviewed source data

source_conflict.action: do not infer facts from conflicting source links
source_conflict.public_output: use unknown when conflict is unresolved

url.http_https_normalization: prefer https when reviewed canonical source supports it
url.trailing_slash_normalization: remove trailing slash except origin root

stale.snapshot_action: include only if lifecycle remains published and public fields remain safe

unknown.snapshot_action: include only if required public fields pass validation

dns_failure.snapshot_action: mark source stale; keep last reviewed public fields if lifecycle remains published
dns_failure.must_not: zero out public scores or invent replacement facts

http_404.snapshot_action: mark source unavailable and require review before future snapshot inclusion

http_410.snapshot_action: treat as removed source and require review before future snapshot inclusion

http_5xx.snapshot_action: temporary source failure; keep last reviewed public fields if still published

malformed_url.snapshot_action: exclude affected source link until corrected

renamed_project.snapshot_action: emit reviewed canonical slug only after redirect or alias review

merged_project.snapshot_action: emit surviving reviewed public record only

source_confidence_boundary.rule: public labels may be emitted only when already reviewed for public display; internal inputs stay private

validation_expectations: Malformed URLs must be excluded until corrected.
Stale source states must not erase last reviewed public fields.
Archived and removed projects are excluded by default.
Private, admin, payment, API key, metering, raw row, Source Confidence internal, and Signal Score internal fields are denied.
No runtime feed, endpoint, DB migration, customer output, external write, or data repository mutation is allowed by PR85.
